steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to explain an advantage of the Hanke-Henry calendar. A strong answer must state what that advantage is and explain why it exists — connecting the calendar's design (364 days, divisible by 7) to the benefit (more predictable scheduling).

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "The Gregorian calendar has 365 days, which is one day longer than the Hanke-Henry permanent calendar.": states a factual comparison of day counts but names no advantage of the Hanke-Henry calendar. ✗
  • "Adopting the Hanke-Henry permanent calendar would help solve a problem with the Gregorian calendar.": hints at an advantage but never identifies the problem or the solution, so no advantage is actually explained. ✗
  • "Designed so calendar dates would occur on the same day of the week each year, the Hanke-Henry calendar supports more predictable scheduling than does the Gregorian calendar.": names the advantage (more predictable scheduling) and explains why (dates fall on the same weekday each year), directly linking cause and benefit. ✓
  • "The Hanke-Henry permanent calendar was developed as an alternative to the Gregorian calendar, which is currently the most-used calendar in the world.": describes the calendar's origins but states no advantage at all. ✗
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
